description_long: This is a whole crab specimen with a robust, rounded carapace and two prominent, asymmetrical claws extending forward. The carapace is dark brown with lighter, mottled patterns, while the legs and claws are a mix of reddish-brown and pale cream hues. The crab's legs are splayed outward, showing fine spines and jointed segments, and the claws are thick and textured, with one noticeably larger than the other. The eyes are set on short stalks at the front of the carapace, and the mouthparts are tucked beneath the head. The underside is not visible, but the dorsal view reveals a hard, segmented exoskeleton typical of decapod crustaceans. The crab is displayed on a flat, white surface next to a color calibration cube for scale.
description: A whole crab with a dark, rounded shell, large asymmetrical claws, and splayed, spiny legs, featuring mottled brown and cream coloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's face, both claws extended forward, and the eyes on short stalks.
description_view_1: Left diagonal view highlighting the left claw and the segmentation of the walking legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the crab's back, carapace, and the arrangement of the legs from behind.
description_view_3: Right diagonal view emphasizing the right claw and the curvature of the body and legs.
